key_insight: PhyNex is an LLM-guided autonomous agent that searches over solution strategies for scorable computational physics tasks while using domain tools to preserve physical consistency.
engineering_relevance: It can reduce manual trial-and-error in scientific software development by helping researchers quickly discover effective algorithms under explicit objectives and constraints.
method: Progressive local search driven by an LLM
Reuses knowledge from both successful and failed attempts
Uses domain-specific computational tools to enforce physical validity
evidence_signal: Across three benchmark problems, PhyNex matched or exceeded human-designed methods, with gains of up to 3.8% in spectral similarity, 15.0% in normalized mean cut, and 5.9% in ergotropy at the 80k checkpoint.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the objective, constraints, and tool integration, so poorly specified tasks could still lead the agent to inefficient or misleading search paths.
paper_title: Large Language Model Based Agent for Automated Discovery in Computational Physics
